steps.thought:
[Observation]
The current screen is the Android home screen. Visible apps include Gmail, Photos, YouTube, Phone, Messages, Chrome, and another Gmail icon in the dock. The Settings app is not visible on this main screen.

[Plan]
Step 1: Open the Settings app → | Step 2: Navigate to Network & internet or Wi-Fi settings | Step 3: Toggle Wi-Fi off, task complete

[Decision]
To turn off Wi-Fi, I need to access the system settings. Since the Settings app is not directly visible on this home screen page, I will use a system-level open action to launch the Settings app directly, which is the most reliable way to proceed.

[Memory]
First action, no prior memory.

[Observation]
The current screen is the Settings main page. Visible options include "Network & internet" (which mentions Mobile, Wi-Fi, hotspot), "Connected devices", "Apps", "Notifications", "Battery", "Storage", and "Sound & vibration".

[Plan Update]
Step 1: Open the Settings app ✓ | Step 2: Navigate to Network & internet or Wi-Fi settings → | Step 3: Toggle Wi-Fi off, task complete

[Decision]
Based on the current plan, the next step is to navigate to the network settings where Wi-Fi controls are located. The "Network & internet" option is clearly visible at the top of the list and its subtitle explicitly includes "Wi-Fi". Therefore, I will tap on "Network & internet" to proceed.

[Memory]
Settings app opened. Target is to turn off Wi-Fi. Next step is within the Network & internet menu.

[Observation]
The current screen is the "Network & internet" settings page. Visible options include "Internet" (Networks available), "Calls & SMS", "SIMs", "Airplane mode" (currently toggled off), "Hotspot & tethering", "Data Saver", and "VPN". There is no direct Wi-Fi toggle switch on this screen; instead, Wi-Fi settings are grouped under the "Internet" option.

[Reflection]
The previous plan assumed we could toggle Wi-Fi off directly in Step 3. However, the current screen requires navigating deeper into the "Internet" menu to find the Wi-Fi toggle.

[Replan]
Step 1: Open Settings ✓ | Step 2: Navigate to Network & internet ✓ | Step 3: Access Internet settings → | Step 4: Toggle Wi-Fi off, task complete

[Decision]
Based on the Replan, the current goal is to access the Internet settings where the Wi-Fi controls are located. The "Internet" option at the top of the list manages available networks, which includes Wi-Fi. Therefore, I will tap on "Internet" to proceed to the screen where I can turn off Wi-Fi.

[Memory]
Target is to turn off Wi-Fi. Currently in Network & internet menu; Wi-Fi settings are nested under the "Internet" option. Airplane mode is off.

[Observation]
The current screen is the "Internet" settings page. The key visible elements include a mobile network connection "T-Mobile" which is connected (3G). Below that, there is a "Wi-Fi" section with a toggle switch that is currently in the ON position (blue). Under Wi-Fi, there is a saved network "AndroidWifi" and an option to "Add network".

[Plan Update]
Step 1: Open Settings ✓ | Step 2: Navigate to Network & internet ✓ | Step 3: Access Internet settings ✓ | Step 4: Toggle Wi-Fi off, task complete →

[Decision]
Based on the Plan Update, the current goal is to toggle Wi-Fi off. The Wi-Fi toggle switch is clearly visible on the screen and is currently turned on. To complete the task of turning Wi-Fi off, I need to tap this toggle switch.

[Memory]
Target is to turn off Wi-Fi. Reached the Internet settings page where the Wi-Fi toggle is located. The toggle is currently on.

[Observation]
The current screen is the "Internet" settings page. The Wi-Fi toggle switch is visible and is now in the OFF position (grayed out). The T-Mobile connection is still active (Connected / 3G). The "Saved networks" section shows 1 network, and there is a note below about Wi-Fi scanning still occurring at any time even when Wi-Fi is off.

[Plan Update]
Step 1: Open Settings ✓ | Step 2: Navigate to Network & internet ✓ | Step 3: Access Internet settings ✓ | Step 4: Toggle Wi-Fi off, task complete ✓

[Decision]
Based on the Plan Update, the final step was to toggle Wi-Fi off. The Observation confirms that the Wi-Fi toggle is now in the off state (gray). The user's instruction "Turn wifi off" has been successfully fulfilled. Therefore, I will declare the task complete.

[Memory]
Target to turn off Wi-Fi has been achieved. Wi-Fi toggle is confirmed in the off state on the Internet settings page. Task is finished.
